Context.purpose: Deliver a shareable end-to-end onboarding flow plus curated-site retrieval: portal onboarding, fixed MCP tool surface (4 proxied + 3 local), worker-based crawl/index pipeline, and attributed evidence retrieval for travel agents.
Context.scope: Keep existing proxied tg-chat tools (`search_messages`, `get_message_context`, `get_related_messages`, `list_sources`), keep `/admin/*` root-token protected, keep portal-first onboarding on `/` and `/portal/*`, and add local curated-site tools (`get_site_sources`, `search_sites`, `get_page_chunk`) backed by PostgreSQL index refreshed by worker crawl/index jobs.

constraints.constraint-1: Public MCP surface remains exactly seven tools: `search_messages`, `get_message_context`, `get_related_messages`, `list_sources` (proxied to upstream), and `get_site_sources`, `search_sites`, `get_page_chunk` (local).
constraints.constraint-2: `search_context`, `validate_google_map_link`, and `verify_tabelog` are removed from active and deferred implementation plans.
constraints.constraint-3: Landing remains intentionally simple and redirects users into user portal; invite link is not required in this phase.
constraints.constraint-4: User portal must support self-serve onboarding through Logto social OAuth only (no email/password auth forms) and avoid manual operator permission grants.
constraints.constraint-5: Portal onboarding must culminate in actionable MCP connection instructions for external AI agents and deterministic OAuth callback handling.
constraints.constraint-6: FastMCP OAuth Proxy is enabled only for `/mcp` transport surface.
constraints.constraint-7: `/admin/*` is protected only by `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` login/session policy.
constraints.constraint-8: Portal session auth on `/portal/*` is isolated from both `/mcp` OAuth and `/admin/*` root session models.
constraints.constraint-9: Curated-source ingestion/indexing is active scope and runs only in worker runtime with crawl-only ingestion policy.
constraints.constraint-10: Billing settings are deferred and represented as future extension points in portal architecture only.
constraints.constraint-11: Usage tracking failures must never block MCP tool execution; tracking is fire-and-forget with warning logs on failure.
constraints.constraint-12: User identity for usage tracking is extracted from MCP OAuth session JWT `sub` claim; the same Logto `sub` links MCP and portal users.
constraints.constraint-13: Unified cross-source search tool is intentionally out of scope; caller must use `search_messages` and `search_sites` explicitly.
constraints.constraint-14: `get_page_chunk` returns bounded excerpts only; full-page verbatim dump is not exposed.
constraints.constraint-15: All Spider and Voyage calls must go through unified proxy (`PROXY_BASE_URL` + `PROXY_SECRET`) with service keys.
constraints.constraint-16: Runtime is split into dedicated API and Worker entrypoints; no single-binary `APP_ROLE` toggle.
constraints.constraint-17: Curated crawling in v1 is crawl-only (scheduled + internal targeted recrawl), with no public manual-refresh endpoint.

contract.purpose: Load and validate runtime configuration for MCP proxy auth/routes, admin auth, portal auth/session, upstream integrations, and database connection.

export-loadConfig.PURPOSE: Validate env and return typed AppConfig.

type-AppConfig.PURPOSE: Typed runtime config for MCP OAuth, portal session, admin auth, upstream clients, and database URL.

notes.note-1: Required env includes existing MCP/admin/logto values plus `PORTAL_SESSION_SECRET`, `LOGTO_M2M_APP_ID`, `LOGTO_M2M_APP_SECRET`, `LOGTO_MCP_USER_ROLE_ID`, `DATABASE_URL`, `VOYAGE_API_KEY`, `SPIDER_API_KEY`, `PROXY_BASE_URL`, and `PROXY_SECRET`.

contract.purpose: Provide structured logs for startup, auth decisions, onboarding routes, tool execution, and upstream failures.

export-createLogger.PURPOSE: Create module-scoped logger with semantic block labels.

contract.purpose: Define deterministic Zod schemas and tool contracts for proxied tg-chat tools and local curated-site tools.

export-SearchMessagesInputPublic.PURPOSE: Public input schema for search_messages with forbidden filters.chat_ids path.

export-SearchSitesInput.PURPOSE: Input schema for search_sites (query, top_k, source_ids).

export-PROXIED_TOOL_NAMES.PURPOSE: Stable allowlist for tool registration and proxy dispatch.

export-TOOL_INPUT_JSON_SCHEMAS.PURPOSE: Tool descriptions used by FastMCP addTool metadata.

contract.purpose: Call tg-chat-rag methods API and normalize protocol/timeouts/errors for proxy handlers.
Replace FastMCP built-in consent screen with portal-styled HTML via monkey-patching OAuthProxy.consentManager.

export-generatePortalConsentScreen.PURPOSE: Render portal-styled consent HTML with scope labels, XSS escaping, and form POST to /oauth/consent.

contract.purpose: Create and configure FastMCP OAuthProxy bound to Logto OIDC endpoints for `/mcp` authentication.

export-createOauthProxy.PURPOSE: Instantiate OAuthProxy with Logto auth/token endpoints, configured scopes, and portal consent patch.

contract.purpose: Wrap identity-provider operations needed by portal social OAuth flows: start URL construction, callback token exchange, identity resolution, and role/permission updates for tester access.

export-buildPortalOauthStartUrl.PURPOSE: Build Logto authorization URL with social direct-sign-in parameters and portal callback target.

export-handlePortalOauthCallback.PURPOSE: Exchange callback authorization code and resolve portal identity profile.

export-ensureMcpAccessRole.PURPOSE: Ensure tester has required MCP access role/policy without manual admin action.

contract.purpose: Validate tool input, apply policy normalization, execute upstream methods, and map deterministic MCP tool responses.

export-executeTool.PURPOSE: Main proxy entrypoint for all registered tools.

contract.purpose: Authenticate admin operators with `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` and protect `/admin` routes via signed cookie session checks.

export-authenticateAdmin.PURPOSE: Validate root token on `/admin/login` and issue session cookie.

export-clearAdminSession.PURPOSE: Invalidate admin auth cookie on logout.

contract.purpose: Issue and validate tester portal session cookies and enforce portal-route authorization checks.

export-authenticatePortalUser.PURPOSE: Create portal session from successful social OAuth callback identity result.

export-requirePortalSession.PURPOSE: Allow or redirect for protected `/portal/*` routes.

contract.purpose: Coordinate first-login provisioning to guarantee tester MCP access entitlements are assigned automatically.

export-provisionTesterAccess.PURPOSE: Ensure identity-provider role/policy grants for MCP testing access are present.

contract.purpose: Create Drizzle PostgreSQL client from DATABASE_URL with fail-fast connectivity probe.

export-createDb.PURPOSE: Validate DATABASE_URL, initialize pg Pool + Drizzle, verify connectivity, and return DbClient.

type-DbClient.PURPOSE: Typed Drizzle database and pg Pool handles for repository modules.

notes.note-1: Reactivated from deprecated status to support M-USAGE-TRACKER persistence. Existing implementation at src/db/index.ts is fully functional.

contract.purpose: Track per-user MCP tool call counts in PostgreSQL and expose query interface for portal display.

param-1.name: UserUsageStats
param-1.type: { tools: { toolName: string; callCount: number; lastCalledAt: Date | null }[]; total: number }

export-createUsageTracker.PURPOSE: Build usage tracker backed by Drizzle PostgreSQL with auto-schema bootstrap.

export-recordToolCall.PURPOSE: Increment per-user per-tool counter via UPSERT; fire-and-forget with warning on failure.

export-getUserStats.PURPOSE: Query all per-tool counters and total for a given user ID.

type-UsageTracker.PURPOSE: Usage tracking interface for tool call recording and stats queries.

type-UserUsageStats.PURPOSE: Per-user usage statistics payload for portal rendering.

schema-usageCountersTable.PURPOSE: Drizzle pg-core schema: usage_counters(user_id, tool_name, call_count, last_called_at) with composite PK.

notes.note-1: Schema uses composite primary key (user_id, tool_name) with UPSERT for atomic counter increment.
notes.note-2: recordToolCall is designed to never throw to callers; failures are caught internally and logged as warnings.

contract.purpose: Serve curated source registry from `site_sources` table with bootstrap seed fallback for initialization/migrations.

export-SITE_SOURCES_RESPONSE.PURPOSE: Bootstrap-only seed constant for initialization/tests (not runtime source of truth).

export-getSiteSources.PURPOSE: Read registry rows from `site_sources` table for local get_site_sources tool.

export-upsertSiteSourcesSeed.PURPOSE: Seed/update initial `site_sources` rows from bootstrap constant.

export-GetSiteSourcesInputSchema.PURPOSE: Empty z.object({}) schema for FastMCP tool registration.

type-SiteSource.PURPOSE: Individual source entry: source_id, name, domain, tier, language, focus, status, crawl_interval_minutes, max_pages.

type-TierDescription.PURPOSE: Tier metadata: tier number, name, focus description.

notes.note-1: Runtime source of truth is PostgreSQL `site_sources`; bootstrap constant is seed-only.
notes.note-2: Tool takes no input parameters; returns registry metadata only (no page/chunk content).
notes.note-3: This is a local tool and must not be added to proxied allowlist.

contract.purpose: Call Spider crawl API through unified proxy for scheduled and targeted recrawl ingestion jobs.

export-runCrawl.PURPOSE: POST Spider crawl request via `${PROXY_BASE_URL}/api.spider.cloud/*` with proxy and provider auth headers.

contract.purpose: Call Voyage embeddings endpoint through unified proxy for document and query embedding paths.
Persist and query curated-site pages, chunks, embeddings, crawl jobs, and active index version metadata.

export-upsertPage.PURPOSE: Insert/update normalized page by canonical URL + content hash.

export-upsertChunks.PURPOSE: Insert/update deterministic chunks with chunking/index versions.

export-upsertEmbeddings.PURPOSE: Store chunk embeddings and embedding metadata for active index version.

export-searchHybrid.PURPOSE: Execute vector + FTS retrieval with active index_version filter.

export-getChunkWithNeighbors.PURPOSE: Fetch bounded chunk excerpt and optional neighbors by chunk_id.

contract.purpose: Normalize Spider payload into canonical URL/title/text records suitable for deterministic chunking.

export-parseCrawlItem.PURPOSE: Extract canonical URL, title, clean text, and fetch metadata.

contract.purpose: Split parsed page text with deterministic structural/token strategy and stable chunk metadata.

export-chunkPage.PURPOSE: Apply v1 split config (`target=450`, `max=650`, `overlap=80`) using npm `tiktoken` token counting and output ordered chunks.

notes.note-1: Token counting strategy is fixed to `tiktoken`; chars/4 approximation is out of scope for v1.

M-SITES-INGESTION.NAME: SitesIngestionOrchestrator

contract.purpose: Run scheduled and targeted crawl-only ingestion jobs and persist parsed/chunked/embedded index artifacts.

export-runScheduledIngestion.PURPOSE: Select due sources by interval and execute crawl ingestion.

export-runTargetedRecrawl.PURPOSE: Execute internal single-URL recrawl via crawl endpoint with strict cap.

M-SITES-INGESTION.depends: M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT, M-SITES-PARSER, M-SITES-CHUNKER, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-LOGGER

contract.purpose: Serve local MCP curated-site search tools with hybrid retrieval and bounded evidence chunk access.

export-searchSites.PURPOSE: Hybrid search over active index_version returning attributed snippets.

export-getPageChunk.PURPOSE: Return bounded excerpt by chunk_id with optional neighbors.

contract.purpose: Render admin login and ops pages with simple root-token auth workflow.

export-renderAdminLayout.PURPOSE: Render HTML layout for admin pages.

M-PORTAL-UI.NAME: PortalUiRoutes

contract.purpose: Render public landing and user-portal pages with per-page route handlers, expose social OAuth entrypoints/callback flow, render MCP setup instructions, and display per-user usage statistics on portal home.

export-handleLandingRequest.PURPOSE: Serve `/` landing with primary redirect action to `/portal`.

export-handlePortalRootRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal` and route to login/home by session state.

export-handlePortalRegisterRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/register` social-provider onboarding page without password fields.

export-handlePortalLoginRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/login` social-provider login page without password fields.

export-handlePortalOauthStartRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/auth/start` and redirect to Logto social OAuth authorization endpoint.

export-handlePortalOauthCallbackRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/auth/callback` code exchange, provisioning, and portal session creation.

export-handlePortalHomeRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/home` authenticated onboarding page with per-user usage statistics.

export-handlePortalAgentSetupRoute.PURPOSE: Handle `GET /portal/integrations/agent-setup` detailed MCP setup guide page.

export-renderPortalLayout.PURPOSE: Render shared portal shell and include compiled Tailwind CSS asset.

export-renderPortalConnectionGuide.PURPOSE: Render MCP endpoint/auth setup instructions for external AI agents.

M-PORTAL-UI.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-PORTAL-AUTH, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING, M-PORTAL-IDENTITY, M-USAGE-TRACKER

notes.note-1: Invite-link flow is intentionally excluded in this phase.
notes.note-2: Portal UI stack is Bun JSX SSR + Tailwind + HTMX, with Alpine.js only for optional local micro-interactions.
notes.note-3: Per-page route architecture is mandatory in this phase (no client-side SPA router).
notes.note-4: Register/login pages are custom but social-only; they route users to OAuth start endpoint rather than collecting password credentials.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.NAME: FastMcpRuntime

contract.purpose: Create FastMCP server with OAuth Proxy for `/mcp`, register fixed tool set (4 proxied + 3 local) with per-user usage tracking, and mount admin + portal HTTP routes on underlying Hono app.

export-createFastMcpRuntime.PURPOSE: Build FastMCP with oauth.enabled=true and mount non-MCP HTTP routes via getApp().

export-registerProxyTools.PURPOSE: Register the four proxied tools with described schemas, canAccess guards, and per-user usage tracking via context.session JWT sub extraction.

export-registerLocalTools.PURPOSE: Register local tools (`get_site_sources`, `search_sites`, `get_page_chunk`) with canAccess guards and per-user usage tracking.

export-extractUserIdFromSession.PURPOSE: Decode JWT sub claim from RuntimeOauthSession idToken or accessToken for usage tracking.

M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TOOLS-CONTRACTS, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-UI, M-USAGE-TRACKER, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SITES-SEARCH

M-SERVER.NAME: ServerMain

contract.purpose: Bootstrap API runtime dependencies and start FastMCP HTTP stream server with MCP, admin, portal, and local curated-site tools.

export-main.PURPOSE: Load config, create API dependencies, mount route handlers, start FastMCP runtime, install graceful shutdown.

M-SERVER.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-AUTH-PROXY, M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT, M-TOOL-PROXY, M-ADMIN-UI, M-PORTAL-IDENTITY, M-PORTAL-AUTH, M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING, M-PORTAL-UI, M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME, M-DB, M-USAGE-TRACKER, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SITES-SEARCH, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT

M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT.NAME: WorkerMain

contract.purpose: Bootstrap worker dependencies and run recurring curated-site scheduling, crawl ingestion, parsing, chunking, and embedding jobs.

export-main.PURPOSE: Load config, initialize ingestion dependencies, run scheduler tick loop, handle graceful shutdown.

M-WORKER-ENTRYPOINT.depends: M-CONFIG, M-LOGGER, M-DB, M-SITE-SOURCES, M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT, M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT, M-SITES-PARSER, M-SITES-CHUNKER, M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY, M-SITES-INGESTION

contract.purpose: Call API entrypoint by default and map fatal startup errors to process exit behavior.

export-bootstrap.PURPOSE: Invoke API main() by default and handle fatal startup exceptions.

DF-LANDING-TO-PORTAL.NAME: public landing redirect flow
DF-LANDING-TO-PORTAL.step-1: Visitor opens `/` and receives concise landing page.
DF-LANDING-TO-PORTAL.step-2: Primary CTA redirects visitor to `/portal` (or directly `/portal/register`).
DF-LANDING-TO-PORTAL.step-3: No invite-link precondition is checked in this phase.

DF-PORTAL-REGISTRATION.NAME: portal registration flow
DF-PORTAL-REGISTRATION.step-1: Tester opens `/portal/register` and chooses a social provider.
DF-PORTAL-REGISTRATION.step-2: Route `/portal/auth/start` builds provider-specific OAuth URL (direct social sign-in) and redirects to Logto.
DF-PORTAL-REGISTRATION.step-3: Logto redirects to `/portal/auth/callback`, then M-PORTAL-IDENTITY exchanges code and resolves identity.
DF-PORTAL-REGISTRATION.step-4: M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING ensures tester MCP access entitlement; M-PORTAL-AUTH issues portal session and redirects to `/portal/home`.

DF-PORTAL-LOGIN.NAME: portal login flow
DF-PORTAL-LOGIN.step-1: Tester opens `/portal/login` and chooses a social provider.
DF-PORTAL-LOGIN.step-2: Route `/portal/auth/start` redirects to provider login via Logto OAuth flow.
DF-PORTAL-LOGIN.step-3: `/portal/auth/callback` resolves identity and M-PORTAL-PROVISIONING re-validates tester access entitlements.
DF-PORTAL-LOGIN.step-4: M-PORTAL-AUTH issues session and portal home renders MCP setup guide.

DF-PORTAL-HTMX-FORMS.NAME: portal progressive form interaction flow
DF-PORTAL-HTMX-FORMS.step-1: User interacts with server-rendered register/login pages containing social provider actions.
DF-PORTAL-HTMX-FORMS.step-2: HTMX requests can update provider selection/error fragments and route to OAuth start endpoint.
DF-PORTAL-HTMX-FORMS.step-3: Route handlers return deterministic HTML fragments/full responses without SPA state management.
DF-PORTAL-HTMX-FORMS.step-4: Alpine.js is used only for optional local UI behaviors that do not replace server authority.

DF-PORTAL-E2E-MCP.NAME: portal to first mcp usage flow
DF-PORTAL-E2E-MCP.step-1: Authenticated portal user reads MCP setup details (`/mcp` endpoint + auth requirements).
DF-PORTAL-E2E-MCP.step-2: User configures external AI agent with provided instructions.
DF-PORTAL-E2E-MCP.step-3: Agent authenticates via OAuth flow on `/mcp` and executes one of active tools (`search_messages` or `search_sites`).

DF-MCP-OAUTH.NAME: mcp oauth proxy auth flow
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-1: External MCP client reaches `/mcp` and receives OAuth flow from FastMCP.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-2: FastMCP OAuthProxy exchanges auth code with Logto upstream.
DF-MCP-OAUTH.step-3: Tool execution proceeds only for authenticated OAuth session.

DF-TOOL-PROXY.NAME: tool proxy flow
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-3: M-TOOL-PROXY applies policy and calls M-TG-CHAT-RAG-CLIENT.
DF-TOOL-PROXY.step-4: Normalized result is returned via MCP transport.

DF-USAGE-TRACKING.NAME: per-user tool call usage tracking flow
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-1: MCP tool call enters execute handler in M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME with context.session containing RuntimeOauthSession.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-2: extractUserIdFromSession decodes idToken (or accessToken) JWT payload and extracts `sub` claim as userId.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-3: After successful tool execution, M-USAGE-TRACKER.recordToolCall(userId, toolName) performs UPSERT on usage_counters table.
DF-USAGE-TRACKING.step-4: If userId extraction or recording fails, tool result is still returned; failure is logged as warning.

DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.NAME: portal usage statistics display flow
DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.step-2: M-PORTAL-UI calls M-USAGE-TRACKER.getUserStats(session.sub) to query per-tool counters.
DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.step-3: Portal home renders usage statistics section with per-tool call counts and total.
DF-PORTAL-USAGE-DISPLAY.step-4: If stats query fails, portal home renders gracefully without usage section or with error placeholder.

DF-SITE-SOURCES.NAME: get_site_sources local tool flow
DF-SITE-SOURCES.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls get_site_sources with no parameters.
DF-SITE-SOURCES.step-2: canAccess guard validates OAuth session (same as proxied tools).
DF-SITE-SOURCES.step-3: Execute handler reads curated source registry from `site_sources` table (seed constant is bootstrap-only).

DF-SITES-INGESTION.NAME: curated sites crawl ingestion flow
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-1: Worker scheduler selects due sources from `site_sources` by `crawl_interval_minutes`.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-2: M-SITES-INGESTION calls M-SPIDER-CLOUD-CLIENT crawl endpoint through unified proxy with source limits.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-3: M-SITES-PARSER normalizes payload into canonical page records.
DF-SITES-INGESTION.step-4: M-SITES-CHUNKER applies deterministic chunking and M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT embeds chunks.

DF-SITES-SEARCH.NAME: search_sites local tool flow
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls `search_sites` with query and optional filters.
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-2: M-SITES-SEARCH builds query embedding via M-VOYAGE-PROXY-CLIENT (`voyage-4`, query mode).
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-3: M-SITES-INDEX-REPOSITORY runs hybrid search (vector + FTS simple config) filtered by active `index_version`.
DF-SITES-SEARCH.step-4: Runtime returns ranked snippets with attribution (`source_id`, `tier`, `original_page_url`, `chunk_id`).

DF-PAGE-CHUNK.NAME: get_page_chunk local tool flow
DF-PAGE-CHUNK.step-2: M-SITES-SEARCH resolves chunk and optional neighbors from repository.
DF-PAGE-CHUNK.step-3: Response returns bounded evidence excerpt with attribution and no full-page dump.

DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.NAME: admin root token flow
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-2: M-ADMIN-AUTH verifies against `ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN` and issues session cookie.
DF-ADMIN-ROOT-AUTH.step-3: Protected `/admin/ops` routes require valid admin session and never use portal/MCP auth paths.

MT-1.NAME: portal onboarding e2e test
MT-1.step-1: Open shared landing URL as unauthenticated user.
MT-1.step-2: Register/login in portal and receive portal session.
MT-1.step-3: Open portal MCP guide and configure external agent.
MT-1.step-4: Execute one active MCP tool successfully.
MT-1.result: Pass when flow works without invite links and without manual admin permission grant.

MT-2.NAME: triple auth isolation test
MT-2.step-1: Use portal session cookie against `/admin/*` and `/mcp`.
MT-2.step-3: Use MCP OAuth token against `/admin/*` and `/portal/*`.
MT-2.result: Pass when each credential works only on its intended surface.

MT-3.NAME: dropped tools surface test
MT-3.step-1: Enumerate MCP tools via runtime metadata.
MT-3.step-3: Verify unified cross-source search tool is absent and exactly seven approved tools remain callable (4 proxied + 3 local).
MT-3.result: Pass when dropped tools do not appear in runtime contracts, plan, or knowledge graph.

MT-4.NAME: usage tracking e2e test
MT-4.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls search_messages; execute handler extracts sub from context.session.idToken JWT.
MT-4.step-2: M-USAGE-TRACKER.recordToolCall(sub, "search_messages") performs UPSERT; usage_counters row created with call_count=1.
MT-4.step-3: Same client calls search_messages again; call_count increments to 2.
MT-4.step-4: Same user opens /portal/home; getUserStats(sub) returns {search_messages: 2, total: 2}; portal renders stats.
MT-4.result: Pass when MCP sub matches portal sub and counters increment correctly across calls.

MT-5.NAME: usage tracking graceful degradation test
MT-5.step-1: MCP client authenticates but idToken is undefined and accessToken is opaque (not JWT).
MT-5.step-2: extractUserIdFromSession returns null; tool execution proceeds normally.
MT-5.step-3: recordToolCall is skipped; warning logged with tool name and reason.
MT-5.step-4: Tool result is returned to MCP client without error.
MT-5.result: Pass when tracking failure never blocks tool execution and warning is emitted.

MT-6.NAME: usage tracker database failure resilience test
MT-6.step-1: PostgreSQL is temporarily unreachable during tool execution.
MT-6.step-2: recordToolCall catches database error internally and logs warning.
MT-6.step-3: Tool result is returned to MCP client without error.
MT-6.step-4: Portal home getUserStats also fails gracefully; page renders without stats or with fallback message.
MT-6.result: Pass when database failures are isolated from both tool execution and portal rendering.

MT-7.NAME: get_site_sources happy path test
MT-7.step-1: Authenticated MCP client calls get_site_sources with empty args.
MT-7.step-2: canAccess passes; execute handler reads registry from `site_sources` table.
MT-7.step-3: Response contains description + tiers and source rows with domain/tier/status/crawl metadata.
MT-7.result: Pass when response matches DB registry state and usage tracking fires.

MT-8.NAME: get_site_sources unauthenticated test
MT-8.step-2: canAccess guard rejects — same behavior as proxied tools.
MT-8.result: Pass when access denied without reaching execute handler.

MT-9.NAME: seven-tool surface completeness test
MT-9.step-1: Enumerate MCP tools via runtime metadata.
MT-9.step-2: Verify all seven tools present: search_messages, get_message_context, get_related_messages, list_sources, get_site_sources, search_sites, get_page_chunk.
MT-9.step-3: Verify first four proxy to upstream and last three execute locally.
MT-9.result: Pass when tool surface is exactly seven tools with correct execution paths.

MT-10.NAME: curated-sites retrieval flow test
MT-10.step-1: Run worker crawl ingestion for at least one active source and persist chunks/embeddings.
MT-10.step-2: Call `search_sites` with a known query and confirm ranked attributed snippets are returned.
MT-10.step-3: Call `get_page_chunk` with returned chunk_id and verify bounded excerpt behavior.
MT-10.result: Pass when local site retrieval path works end-to-end without direct runtime crawl calls.

MT-11.NAME: crawl-only policy test
MT-11.step-1: Attempt internal targeted recrawl for one URL.
MT-11.step-2: Verify ingestion uses Spider crawl path with strict cap, not scrape/manual endpoint.
MT-11.step-3: Verify no public MCP tool exposes manual refresh action.
MT-11.result: Pass when v1 crawl-only policy is enforced in both contracts and runtime behavior.

Phase-1.name: ScopeAndContractsAlignment
Phase-1.step-1: Synchronize requirements/technology/development-plan/knowledge-graph with portal-first scope and dropped tools list.
Phase-1.step-2: Extend config contract with portal session requirements.
Phase-1.justification: Architecture and contracts must reflect new product direction before module generation.

Phase-2.name: PortalIdentityAndAuthCore
Phase-2.step-1: Implement M-PORTAL-IDENTITY integration for social OAuth start/callback/provisioning endpoints.
Phase-2.step-2: Implement M-PORTAL-AUTH session model for tester portal routes.
Phase-2.justification: Self-serve onboarding depends on identity integration and deterministic provisioning core logic.

Phase-3.name: PortalAndLandingUI
Phase-3.step-1: Implement simple landing with redirect CTA to `/portal`.
Phase-3.step-3: Implement Bun JSX SSR templates/components and compile Tailwind shared stylesheet asset.
Phase-3.step-4: Add HTMX interactions for social-provider UI states and OAuth start actions; use Alpine.js only for optional local micro-interactions.
Phase-3.justification: UI layer is meaningful only after auth/provisioning contracts are defined and must follow the approved lightweight SSR interaction model.

Phase-4.name: RuntimeMountingAndE2EValidation
Phase-4.step-1: Mount landing and portal routes in M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME alongside existing admin routes.
Phase-4.step-2: Add integration/smoke tests for onboarding e2e and auth isolation.
Phase-4.step-3: Confirm proxied tg-chat tool surface remains fixed to four active tools.
Phase-4.justification: Runtime composition and tests validate real product objective: shareable tester onboarding to first MCP success.

Phase-5.name: UsageTrackingAndPortalDisplay
Phase-5.step-1: Add `DATABASE_URL` to M-CONFIG AppConfig type and loadConfig validation.
Phase-5.step-2: Reactivate M-DB: wire createDb into M-SERVER bootstrap with fail-fast connectivity probe.
Phase-5.step-3: Implement M-USAGE-TRACKER: Drizzle schema (usage_counters table), createUsageTracker factory, recordToolCall UPSERT, getUserStats query.
Phase-5.step-4: Add extractUserIdFromSession helper in M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME: decode JWT payload from idToken/accessToken to extract sub claim.
Phase-5.step-5: Update registerProxyTools execute handlers to accept context parameter, extract userId, and call usageTracker.recordToolCall after successful execution.
Phase-5.step-6: Update M-PORTAL-UI handlePortalHomeRoute to query usageTracker.getUserStats and render per-tool call counts and total on portal home.
Phase-5.step-7: Wire M-DB and M-USAGE-TRACKER into M-SERVER dependency bootstrap and pass usageTracker to runtime and portal handler deps.
Phase-5.justification: Usage tracking requires database persistence (M-DB reactivation), a new data-layer module (M-USAGE-TRACKER), JWT identity extraction in tool execution (M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME), and portal display integration (M-PORTAL-UI). Steps are ordered by dependency: config first, then DB, then tracker, then runtime integration, then UI.

Phase-6.name: CuratedSitesContractsAndSchema
Phase-6.step-2: Add required site index schema/migrations (`site_sources`, `site_pages`, `site_chunks`, `site_chunk_embeddings`, `site_crawl_jobs`) with index_version support, execute `CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS vector;`, and define `site_chunk_embeddings.embedding` as `vector(1024)` for `voyage-4` similarity search.
Phase-6.step-3: Switch M-SITE-SOURCES runtime read path to `site_sources` table while keeping bootstrap constant as seed-only.
Phase-6.step-4: Extend tool contracts for local `search_sites` and `get_page_chunk` schemas.
Phase-6.justification: Contract and schema alignment must be complete before worker ingestion or local retrieval tools can be implemented safely.

Phase-7.name: RuntimeSplitAndIngestionWorker
Phase-7.step-1: Introduce dedicated worker entrypoint and startup wiring separate from API runtime.
Phase-7.step-2: Implement Spider proxy crawl client and deterministic crawl-only ingestion orchestrator.
Phase-7.step-3: Implement parser/chunker/embedding pipeline (`voyage-4`) and repository upserts for active index version.
Phase-7.step-4: Implement scheduling cadence (5-min tick, tier-based intervals, weekly discovery pass) with jitter.
Phase-7.justification: Worker isolation avoids API interference and enables predictable ingestion lifecycle and crawl budget enforcement.

Phase-8.name: CuratedSitesLocalTools
Phase-8.step-1: Register local `search_sites` and `get_page_chunk` tools in M-FASTMCP-RUNTIME (not in proxied allowlist).
Phase-8.step-2: Implement M-SITES-SEARCH hybrid retrieval path and bounded chunk evidence response.
Phase-8.step-3: Keep tool model explicit (`search_messages` + `search_sites`) and reject unified search abstraction.
Phase-8.justification: This phase exposes user-facing curated retrieval while preserving clear tool boundaries and attribution guarantees.

Phase-9.name: OpsHardening
Phase-9.step-1: Add ingestion observability, retry/backoff policy, and provider outage fallback behavior.
Phase-9.step-2: Add takedown/pause controls and strict bounded-output checks for evidence endpoints.
Phase-9.step-3: Stabilize cost/performance via source budgets and cadence tuning.
Phase-9.justification: Operational controls are required for production reliability, safety, and predictable operating cost.

RiskAssessment.risk-1: Automatic provisioning can fail due to identity-provider API/permission misconfiguration, blocking onboarding despite valid registration.
RiskAssessment.risk-2: Auth-boundary mistakes across `/mcp`, `/admin/*`, and `/portal/*` can create privilege crossover.
RiskAssessment.risk-3: Portal session secret/ttl misconfiguration can cause unstable login UX or accidental long-lived sessions.
RiskAssessment.risk-4: Social connector misconfiguration or provider outage can break both registration and login flows simultaneously.
RiskAssessment.risk-5: UI/contract drift between portal setup instructions and actual MCP OAuth requirements may increase tester setup failures.
RiskAssessment.risk-6: Deprecated modules (M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER, M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER, M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY) remaining in repo can confuse future implementation unless graph linkage remains explicit.
RiskAssessment.risk-7: MCP OAuth session JWT (idToken/accessToken) may not always contain decodable sub claim depending on Logto configuration, causing silent tracking gaps.
RiskAssessment.risk-8: PostgreSQL unavailability during tool execution causes usage tracking gaps but does not affect tool functionality due to fire-and-forget design.
RiskAssessment.risk-9: Spider provider outage or quota exhaustion can stall freshness for curated-site index.
RiskAssessment.risk-10: Improper crawl limits or missing jitter can cause bursty load and unstable ingestion latency.
RiskAssessment.risk-11: Index-version misconfiguration can mix old/new embeddings and degrade ranking consistency.
RiskAssessment.risk-12: Bounded excerpt policy regressions in get_page_chunk can accidentally expose oversized content.

M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.NAME: McpAuthProvider
M-MCP-AUTH-PROVIDER.reason: Replaced by FastMCP OAuth Proxy integration for `/mcp`.

M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.NAME: McpAuthAdapter
M-MCP-AUTH-ADAPTER.reason: No longer needed when FastMCP OAuth Proxy handles OAuth session flow.

M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.NAME: ApiKeyRepository
M-API-KEY-REPOSITORY.reason: Out of active onboarding scope; admin remains ROOT_AUTH_TOKEN-only.

M-SEARCH-CONTEXT-TOOL.NAME: SearchContextTool
M-SEARCH-CONTEXT-TOOL.reason: Dropped from product plan by scope decision; not implemented in active roadmap.

M-VERIFY-GMAP-TOOL.NAME: ValidateGoogleMapLinkTool
M-VERIFY-GMAP-TOOL.reason: Dropped from product plan by scope decision; not implemented in active roadmap.

M-VERIFY-TABELOG-TOOL.NAME: VerifyTabelogTool
M-VERIFY-TABELOG-TOOL.reason: Dropped from product plan by scope decision; not implemented in active roadmap.

M-UNIFIED-SEARCH-TOOL.NAME: UnifiedSearchTool
M-UNIFIED-SEARCH-TOOL.reason: Intentionally out of scope; product keeps explicit `search_messages` and `search_sites` tools.
